seoTitle: How to Take a Partial Profit on a TradingView Position
metaDescription: Learn how to take a partial profit in TradingView by closing part of an open position, using the paper trading feature to practice locking in gains.
intro: Locking in some gains while letting the rest of a trade run is a core risk management move, and TradingView makes it possible directly from an open position. This guide shows how to use TradingView's paper trading feature to practice closing part of a position without touching the whole trade. It's aimed at traders who want to secure profit on a winner while still capturing further upside.

steps.heading: Open your chart and positions panel
steps.body: Log in to TradingView, load your chart, and click Paper Trading to practice trades without financial risk.

steps.heading: Select the position you want to partially close
steps.body: Click Positions to view your active trades, then choose the specific position you want to lock in profit on.

steps.heading: Find the partial close option
steps.body: Click on the position to bring up its menu, then look for the partial close option under the order actions.

steps.heading: Set how much of the position to close
steps.body: Toggle partial close and enter the portion of the trade you want to exit, keeping the rest open based on your risk management plan.

steps.heading: Confirm the partial close
steps.body: Click Partially Close to secure that portion of your profit while the remaining position stays open to capture further movement.

faq.question: Can I take partial profit on a live TradingView trade, not just paper trading?
faq.answer: The same partial close option is available on connected live broker accounts, though the paper trading feature is the safest way to practice the steps first.

faq.question: How do I decide how much of my position to close?
faq.answer: That comes down to your own risk management and profit targets; some traders close half, others close a smaller portion to keep more upside exposure.

faq.question: Does partially closing a position cost extra fees?
faq.answer: Any fees depend on your connected broker's commission structure, since TradingView itself doesn't charge separately for the partial close action.

faq.question: What happens to my stop loss after I partially close a position?
faq.answer: Your existing stop loss typically still applies to the remaining open portion, though you may want to adjust it manually to match your new position size.
